meta.title: Verbal Memory Decline after Temporal Lobe Resection
meta.outcome: Predicted probability of clinically significant delayed verbal-memory decline after temporal lobe resection (TLR) for epilepsy. This calculator implements the RAVLT Delay model (word-list learning; c = 0.81), the strongest of the three models in Busch 2021.
meta.cff_info:
What this shows. This tool estimates the probability of a clinically significant decline in delayed verbal memory after temporal lobe resection (TLR) for epilepsy, using preoperative variables available in most surgical centers (Busch RM et al., Neurology 2021). Models were developed in 359 adults at the Cleveland Clinic and externally validated in 290 adults across 5 US/Canadian centers.

Key findings

  • Busch 2021 built three models, one per delayed-memory measure: RAVLT Delay (Rey Auditory Verbal Learning Test word list; c = 0.81), Logical Memory / LM Delay (WMS-III story recall; c = 0.76) and Verbal Paired Associates / VPA Delay (WMS-III word pairs; c = 0.78).
  • Across all three measures, surgery in the dominant (usually left) hemisphere and a higher preoperative memory score raised the odds of postoperative decline.
  • The RAVLT model additionally included hippocampal resection (resected → higher risk); the LM and VPA models additionally included years of education (more education → lower risk).
  • Overall, 29% of the development cohort and 26% of the validation cohort declined on at least one measure. Calibration was very good (no systematic over- or under-estimation).

What this calculator implements

This calculator implements the RAVLT Delay model — the most discriminating of the three (c = 0.81) — with predictors surgery side, baseline RAVLT Delay raw score and hippocampal resection. The LM and VPA models (which instead use baseline score, surgery side and education) are described above but not computed here.

How to read it

  • The result is the percent probability of a clinically significant RAVLT delayed-recall decline (a raw drop of ≥4 points on retest, per published reliable-change indices).
  • Higher baseline memory, dominant-hemisphere surgery and hippocampal resection each push the probability up.

Demonstration tool for preoperative counseling — an aid to shared decision-making, not a substitute for formal neuropsychological evaluation.

predictors.name: Surgery side
predictors.assess: Side of temporal lobe resection relative to language dominance (dominant is usually the left hemisphere, established by fMRI and/or Wada). Dominant-hemisphere resection markedly raises the risk of verbal-memory decline (RAVLT model coefficient 2.635; OR 13.94).

predictors.name: Baseline RAVLT Delay score
predictors.assess: Preoperative Rey Auditory Verbal Learning Test (RAVLT) delayed-recall raw score — number of words recalled after the 30-minute delay, from a 15-word list (range 0-15). A higher preoperative score means more memory to lose and raises the odds of decline (coefficient 0.246 per point; OR 1.28).

predictors.name: Hippocampal resection
predictors.assess: Whether the hippocampus was resected (vs spared) at surgery. Hippocampal resection substantially increases the risk of delayed verbal-memory decline (RAVLT model coefficient 2.160; OR 8.67).

model.note: RAVLT Delay logistic model (Busch 2021, Table 2; c = 0.81): logit = -7.068 + 0.246·(baseline RAVLT Delay) + 2.635·(dominant surgery) + 2.160·(hippocampus resected). The companion LM Delay (c = 0.76) and VPA Delay (c = 0.78) models use baseline score, surgery side and education instead (see About) and are not computed here.
model.viz_info: Each preoperative factor adds a weight (the published model coefficient) to a running total. Their sum plus the model constant is passed through a logistic function to give the percent probability of delayed verbal-memory decline. The waterfall shows how each factor pushes the estimate up or down; the donut shows the resulting probability.

provenance.source: Busch RM et al. 2021 Neurology 97:e263-e274 — Table 2 (Model Coefficients and ORs: published logistic coefficients + intercepts for RAVLT, LM, VPA). doi 10.1212/WNL.0000000000012221, PMID 34011574.
provenance.notes: RAVLT Delay model implemented (primary; highest c = 0.81). Coefficients from Table 2: baseline score 0.246, dominant surgery 2.635, hippocampus resected 2.160, intercept -7.068. Continuous baseline RAVLT delayed-recall raw score (0-15 word list). LM (intercept -2.123; baseline 0.357, dominant 1.339, education -0.256) and VPA (intercept -3.398; baseline 0.310, dominant 1.663, education -0.145) models NOT encoded because their predictor coefficients differ from RAVLT and the single-coef-per-predictor logistic form cannot carry per-output betas; described in About text. Sanity checks: nondominant/spared/low-baseline -> ~0.2%; dominant+resected+baseline 8 -> ~42%; dominant+resected+baseline 12 -> ~66%; directions match paper. Extracted 2026-07-08.
